evidence.excerpt: Using these annotations as foundation, we prompt GPT-4.1 and o4-mini to generate thinking traces for each capability-targeted task.
evidence.explanation: GPT-4.1 and o4-mini are used to generate Math/Code Meta-Reasoning thinking traces.

evidence.excerpt: we 1) programmatically generate these problems, 2) distill thinking traces from GPT-4.1 and o4-mini models, and 3) finally filter those for correctness using an output verifier (Python programs).
evidence.explanation: GPT-4.1 and o4-mini distill thinking traces for Program-Verifiable; verifier (Python) filters them.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: QwQ-32B is the source model whose synthetic thinking traces (QwQ Reasoning Traces, 1.87B tokens) are filtered and included in the midtraining mix consumed by allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B.

evidence.excerpt: QWQ Reasoning Traces ... 1.87B (1.87%) ... ^=filtering or light transformation of existing external data
evidence.explanation: QwQ Reasoning Traces are existing thinking traces produced by QwQ-32B (the QwQ model is the generator), filtered for inclusion.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: CraneMath (a midtraining sub-corpus of allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B) is generated by rewriting FineMath4+ (Allal et al., 2025) with the SwallowMath prompt (off-lattice source dataset mention).

evidence.excerpt: rewriting FineMath4+ with the SwallowMath prompt ... we collect the Megamath-Web-Pro data occurring after June 2023 ... we created a fully-open replica of SwallowCode ... starting with just the python files from The Stack v2 Smol ... we adopt the TinyGSM protocol (Liu et al., 2023a) and prompts to generate 100 new problems for each existing MATH problem
evidence.explanation: Names the raw source datasets that synthetic midtraining sub-corpora are derived from.

evidence.excerpt: we generate an independent reproduction of SwallowMath by rewriting FineMath4+ with the SwallowMath prompt, using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a) for generation. We denote this new mix as CraneMath
evidence.explanation: States Qwen3 was used as the rewriter to produce CraneMath from FineMath4+.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: TinyMATH (PoT/Mind) sub-corpora used by allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B during midtraining are seeded from the 7500 examples in the MATH training set, with 100 new problems generated per item via the TinyGSM protocol (off-lattice mention).

evidence.excerpt: rewriting FineMath4+ with the SwallowMath prompt ... we collect the Megamath-Web-Pro data occurring after June 2023 ... we created a fully-open replica of SwallowCode ... starting with just the python files from The Stack v2 Smol ... we adopt the TinyGSM protocol (Liu et al., 2023a) and prompts to generate 100 new problems for each existing MATH problem
evidence.explanation: Names the raw source datasets that synthetic midtraining sub-corpora are derived from.

evidence.excerpt: we adopt the TinyGSM protocol (Liu et al., 2023a) and prompts to generate 100 new problems for each existing MATH problem, and then generate pythonic solutions for each of these new problems.
evidence.explanation: TinyMATH adapts the TinyGSM protocol to the MATH dataset.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Gemma 3 4B served as reference long-context model for LongPpl-based key-token filtering ablations over the Longmino Mix; the filter did not outperform gzip and was not used in the final long-context run for either Olmo 3 size.

evidence.excerpt: We compute LongPpl over 10B tokens of Dolma 3 Longmino Mix using Gemma 3 4B as the reference model
evidence.explanation: Records Gemma 3 4B's role in the ablation-only LongPpl filtering experiment.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Olmo 3 32B Base training data is filtered (decontaminated) to remove sequences derived from OlmoBaseEval evaluation tasks.

evidence.excerpt: Our goal is to call whether any training sequence t is derived directly from an eval sequence e.
evidence.explanation: decon decontaminates training data against eval tasks for 32B as well.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Stage-1 32B pretraining draws from the dolma3 pool, with the 0925 manifest using all-dressed-snazzy2 web organizer vigintile splits in place of the 0625 common_crawl topic splits.

evidence.explanation: Documents that the 0925-official manifest sources from dolma3.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: 0925-official manifest includes a finemath-3plus split (FineMath 3+ subset of HuggingFaceTB/finemath, fed through OLMo's manifest naming).

evidence.explanation: Confirms finemath-3plus split in 0925 manifest.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: In-loop evaluator runs ARC challenge bpb_5shot and mc_5shot_fast every 1000 steps during stage-1 32B pretraining.

evidence.explanation: ARC Challenge tasks in 32B stage-1 evals.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: In-loop evaluator runs ARC easy bpb_5shot and mc_5shot_fast.

evidence.explanation: ARC Easy in 32B stage-1 evals.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: 32B pretraining recipe (data mixes 0925-official, 0925-ingredient1/2-100B, longmino-mix-0925) is a refined revision of the 7B 0625 recipe (mixes 0625-official, midtraining-mix-0625-100B, longmino-mix-0625) with similar component composition but updated ratios and olmocr_science_pdfs paths.

evidence.excerpt: we recommend the mixes defined for Olmo 3 32B as they are slightly more refined
evidence.explanation: README states 32B mixes are refined version of 7B.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: General Reasoning Mix (1.87B tokens, 1.87% of the mix) is a compilation of GeneralThought-430K, OpenThoughts-114k, and Open-R1-Math-220k thinking traces.

evidence.excerpt: General reasoning mix is a compilation of three existing datasets: GeneralThought-430K, OpenThoughts-114k... and Open-R1-Math-220k
evidence.explanation: General Reasoning Mix combines three named source datasets.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Dolmino 1 Flan (5.0B tokens, 5% of the mix) is the same FLAN v2 subset and preprocessing used in Olmo 2.

evidence.excerpt: Flan ... included a subset of the Flan dataset in the final mix. We use same subset and preprocessing from OLMo 2
evidence.explanation: Identifies Dolmino 1 Flan as the FLAN v2 subset reused from OLMo 2.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B

evidence.excerpt: For each of the 7500 examples in the MATH training set, we generate 100 new, similar problems.
evidence.explanation: MATH training set is the seed source for TinyMATH.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Olmo 3 Base 32B's Wikipedia & Wikibooks sub-corpora are sourced from Dolma v1 (allenai/dolma) processing.

evidence.explanation: Dolma v1 supplies the Wikipedia/Wikibooks slice consumed by Olmo 3 Base 32B.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Olmo 3 Base 32B's CraneMath, MegaMatt, and CraneCode synthetic midtraining sub-corpora were generated by rewriting source web data (FineMath 4+, MegaMath-Web-Pro filtered, the-stack-v2-smol Python) with Qwen3 (size unspecified in source).

evidence.excerpt: rewriting FineMath4+ with the SwallowMath prompt, using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a) for generation. We denote this new mix as CraneMath
evidence.explanation: Qwen3 generates the CraneMath/MegaMatt/CraneCode synthetic data consumed by Olmo 3 Base 32B midtraining.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Olmo 3 Base 32B's Dolmino 1 Flan midtraining sub-corpus reuses a subset of the Flan dataset with the same preprocessing as OLMo 2.

evidence.excerpt: Flan Through microanneals, we also find the Flan dataset (Wei et al., 2021; Longpre et al., 2023) improves performance ... we use same subset and preprocessing from OLMo 2
evidence.explanation: Flan dataset inspires Dolmino 1 Flan consumed by Olmo 3 Base 32B.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Olmo 3 Base 32B's long-context recipe development was informed by surveying token-efficient extension recipes including LongAttn (Wu et al., 2025) which selects long-range-dependency documents via self-attention scores.

evidence.excerpt: LongAttn (Wu et al., 2025b) constructs a 5B-token corpus using self-attention scores from existing language models
evidence.explanation: LongAttn cited as a recipe reference shaping Olmo 3 Base 32B long-context development.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: OlmoBaseEval Easy suite is used as a proxy metric for ablations during Olmo 3 Base 32B development decisions.

evidence.excerpt: We propose a Base Easy task suite which measures bits-per-byte (BPB) over tasks from the Base Main suite that have gold labels or human-written answers ... we use the small-scale OlmoBaseEval Easy suite as a proxy-metric for making data decisions.
evidence.explanation: Section 3.3.2 establishes that the OlmoBaseEval Easy suite (BPB form) is used as a proxy metric for ablation/development decisions.

evidence.excerpt: The resulting OlmoBaseEval consists of a Base Easy suite for making development decisions using small compute budgets (e.g., less than 1B parameters) and a Base Main suite for development decisions for the final pretraining run and midtraining.
evidence.explanation: Defines that the Base Easy suite is the ablation-driving proxy.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B
edges.description: Midtraining ingredient 1 mix includes gemini_reasoning thinking-traces generated by Gemini-2.5 at ~0.25% of the mix.

evidence.explanation: Yaml exposes the gemini_reasoning thinking-traces source.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1125-32B

evidence.explanation: OLMo 2 32B is the immediate predecessor at 32B scale.
